items.title: Cortina’s 70-Year-Old Curling Stadium Is a Star at the Winter Olympics

items.title: Syrian Army Crosses New Threshold With Advances in Kurdish Regions
items.description: The advance of the Syrian Army into Kurdish regions could put nearly all the country under one authority. But it ends a dream of autonomy for ethnic K...

items.title: Iran’s Protests Have Ended, but the Anger and Pain Haven’t
items.description: In the aftermath of another wave of antigovernment unrest, Iran is gripped by a mood of collective grief and uncertainty about the future.

items.title: A Russian ‘Village of Military Valor’ Waits for Its Reward

items.title: Prices Jump as Venezuelans Abroad Consider Buying Property Back Home
items.description: Nicolás Maduro’s capture and talk of oil investment have pushed prices higher, even as brokers say enthusiasm is outpacing demand in a weak economy.
